correctness.justification: All 24 acceptance tests pass. Every specified requirement is implemented correctly: single '@' check, local part validation (dots, consecutive dots, allowed chars), domain validation (labels, hyphen rules, TLD alphabetic 2+ chars), length limit of 254 chars.

edge_cases.justification: All edge cases handled: empty string returns False, exactly 254 chars valid, 255 chars invalid, leading/trailing dots in local, consecutive dots, missing local part, domain with no dot, leading dot in domain, hyphen at start/end of label, single-char TLD, numeric TLD, empty labels from double dots in domain.

code_quality.justification: Excellent structure with pre-compiled regex constants, full type hints, clean helper function decomposition, idiomatic Python. Minor point: _LABEL_ALLOWED regex uses alternation that could be slightly cleaner, but overall very readable and well-organized.

documentation.justification: Comprehensive module-level docstring explaining scope and RFC 5321 non-compliance. The validate_email function has a full NumPy-style docstring with Parameters, Returns, and Examples sections including both valid and invalid cases. Private helper functions have concise but clear one-line docstrings. Inline comments explain regex patterns and constants. Only minor gap: private helpers lack parameter/return documentation.

test_coverage.justification: Thorough test suite with 26 tests covering valid emails (7 variants), structural failures (4), local part failures (4), domain failures (7), length boundary conditions (2), and error propagation simulation (2). Well-organized with clear section comments. Minor gap: no tests for local part length limits or very long individual domain labels.

robustness.justification: Validates all key inputs: empty string check, length limit, exact '@' count, non-empty local/domain, all domain labels non-empty. Pre-compiled regex avoids runtime compilation overhead. Exceptions propagate naturally rather than being silently swallowed. Minor gap: no explicit handling for non-string inputs (e.g., None would raise AttributeError).
